div.head:
No. 414
The Ambassador in Iraq (Crocker) to the Department of StateRepeated to Tel Aviv, Cairo, Beirut, Damascus, Tripoli, Benghazi, Jidda, Amman, and Jerusalem.
div.p:
726. Depcirtel 758, March [4]. Emb has observed little or no change indicating relaxation Iraq–Israel tension. Nuri has repeatedly stressed, probably sincerely, (1) his conception collective security pact as being defensive without aggressive intention re Israel, (2) his view that pact cld be basis for wider defense arrangements against external threat (Embdesp 822 Mar 5Not printed; it described a conversation with Iraqi Prime Minister Nuri Pasha on the question of Middle East defense. (780.5/3–552)). Such statements, however, shld not be interpreted as indicating real decrease basis
Iraq antagonism to Israel. (Incidentally Zafrullah’s remark re Israel severely criticized by press here.)
In Emb’s view, 95 percent Iraq people still regard Israel as number one danger rather than USSR. Any change or developments will be reported fully.
